candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, fold $\triangle ABC$ along the segment $DE$ so that point $B$ falls on point $F$; if $AC \parallel DE$, $\angle A = 70^\circ$, $AB = AC$, then what is the degree measure of $\angle CEF$?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution}: We fold triangle $ABC$ along the line segment $DE$, such that point $B$ falls on point $F$,\\
thus, triangle $BDE \cong FDE$,\\
hence, $\angle DEB = \angle DEF$,\\
since $\angle A=70^\circ$, and $AB=AC$,\\
thus, $\angle B = \angle C = \frac{1}{2} \times (180^\circ - 70^\circ) = 55^\circ$,\\
since $AC \parallel DE$,\\
hence, $\angle DEB = \angle C = 55^\circ = \angle DEF$,\\
thus, $\angle FEC = 180^\circ - \angle DEB - \angle DEF = \boxed{70^\circ}$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集被视为具备较稳定的多解潜力，可进入更强的多解挖掘链路。
